blockingIssues.description: Host edit and repair flows auto-select the first available identity when the linked identity is missing or blocked, so saving unrelated changes can silently relink the host to the wrong identity. The same silent-relink failure was independently reported in `keystore-secret-persistence-and-recovery`.

After deleting a linked identity, host repair infers auth mode from remaining identities instead of preserving the previous credential family, so a formerly key-based host can reopen in password mode and preselect an unrelated password identity.

Imported-key passphrase state is inferred from whether the user typed text into the passphrase field rather than from the parsed key material, so replacing an unencrypted key can incorrectly persist `hasPassphrase=true` and store a bogus passphrase.
Canceling host delete confirmation rebuilds the editor from persisted data instead of the in-progress draft, discarding unsaved edits made before entering the confirmation flow.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that imported-key flows accept OpenSSH and PEM private keys in `.factory/library/architecture.md`.
Documented the `SecretStorageState` lifecycle (`AVAILABLE`, `MISSING`, `BLOCKED`) and the requirement to gate auth flows on `Identity.isAuthenticationReady` in `.factory/library/architecture.md`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a host/identity repair invariant: when a linked identity is missing or blocked, repair flows must preserve the previous auth-family intent and require an explicit replacement choice; they must never auto-select another identity or infer a different auth mode from whatever identities remain.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three independent reviews (`identity-password-library-and-validation`, `identity-key-generation-and-repair-flows`, and `keystore-secret-persistence-and-recovery`) all found host edit/repair paths silently switching identity or auth mode when the original link was stale, deleted, or blocked.
